content:
| Property | Value | Relevance to Laser Cleaning |
|---|---|---|
| Melting Point | 950-1080 °C (depending on alloy composition) | Determines maximum permissible temperature during ablation to avoid melting or phase changes. |
| Thermal Conductivity | 110 W/m·K (approximate, varies with alloy) | Influences heat dissipation and prevents damage to the substrate.  Higher conductivity facilitates faster cooling. |
| Optical Absorption Coefficient (at 1064 nm) | 10<sup>4</sup> - 10<sup>5</sup> cm<sup>-1</sup> (approximate, depends on alloy & surface condition) | Dictates the depth of laser penetration and efficiency of ablation. Higher absorption enhances cleaning. |
| Surface Reflectivity (at 1064 nm) | 0.1-0.6 (approximate, depends on alloy & surface condition) |  Lower reflectivity increases absorption and improves cleaning efficiency. |
|
